concept: Focus on constructing clearer and more complete sentences to enhance communication. This will help avoid confusion and make conversations flow better.
common_mistakes: Omitting subjects or verbs in sentences
Using repetitive phrases like 'I have a lot of' without variation
Incorrect word order or missing articles
tips_to_remember: Always include a subject and a verb in your sentences.
Try to vary your vocabulary to avoid repetition.
Use articles ('a', 'an', 'the') correctly to clarify your meaning.

examples.text: I have many lessons to prepare as a computer science teacher.
My day was busy, but I enjoyed teaching my students.
I discussed Python and how to manage lists and dictionaries.
I like to listen to music while I run errands.

mini_task.instruction: Describe your day using at least five complete sentences.
success_checklist: Each sentence has a subject and a verb.

concept: Learn synonyms and related phrases to express ideas more clearly and avoid repetition in conversation.
common_mistakes: Using the same words repeatedly (e.g., 'a lot', 'busy')
Not knowing alternative expressions for common ideas
tips_to_remember: Keep a list of synonyms for common words you use.
Practice using new vocabulary in sentences.
Incorporate descriptive language to make your speech more engaging.

examples.text: I have numerous tasks to complete today.
My schedule is quite hectic, but manageable.
I find Python to be an accessible programming language.
Listening to music makes running errands more enjoyable.

mini_task.instruction: Choose three common words you often use and find synonyms for them. Use each synonym in a sentence.
success_checklist: Identify three synonyms for each word.
Use each synonym correctly in a sentence.
Ensure sentences are clear and meaningful.

concept: Engage in back-and-forth dialogue to improve fluency and comfort in spontaneous conversation.
common_mistakes: Giving one-word answers instead of elaborating
tips_to_remember: Try to elaborate on your answers to keep the conversation going.
Ask follow-up questions to show interest and encourage dialogue.

examples.text: What do you enjoy most about teaching?
Can you tell me more about your favorite anime?
How do you usually spend your weekends?
What challenges do your students face when learning programming?

mini_task.instruction: Pair up with a friend or language partner and have a 2-minute conversation about your favorite hobbies, taking turns asking questions.
success_checklist: Ask at least two follow-up questions.

concept: Learn to use fillers and natural speech patterns to sound more fluent and less robotic during conversations.
common_mistakes: Speaking too formally in casual conversations
Not using contractions (e.g., 'I am' instead of 'I'm')
tips_to_remember: Use fillers sparingly to give yourself time to think.
Practice speaking in a more relaxed, conversational tone.
Incorporate contractions to sound more natural.

examples.text: So, I was thinking about what to do this weekend.
You know, I really enjoy watching anime.
I mean, Python is such a cool language to teach.
Like, I usually listen to music while I work.

mini_task.instruction: Record yourself talking about your favorite movie or show for 2 minutes, using fillers naturally.
success_checklist: Use fillers appropriately without overdoing it.
Include contractions to sound more natural.
